title: psoralea retrorsa
intro: Psoralea retrorsa, also known as Indian turnip or fetid glider, is a plant species native to eastern Australia, characterized by its unique, bitter-tasting root. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Psoralea retrorsa include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas or infusions, and it has also been used in some regions as a food source, often roasted or boiled as a vegetable.
